title: Method and apparatus for dynamic electrical load sensing and line to load switching
abstract: A switch has a first input to couple to an AC electrical power source, a second input to couple to a DC electrical power source, and an output to couple to an electrical power load. A control module transmits a signal to the electrical power load to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power, and receives in response thereto an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power. The control module then transmits a signal to the switch to configure the switch to receive electrical power from one of the AC electrical power source and the DC electrical power source and transmit the received electrical power to the electrical power load, responsive to the received indication.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus, comprising: a switch having a first input to couple to an AC electrical power source, a second input to couple to a DC electrical power source, and an output to couple to an electrical power load; a control module to transmit a signal to the electrical power load to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power; the control module to receive an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal; and the control module to transmit a signal to switch to configure the switch to receive electrical power from one of the AC electrical power source and the DC electrical power source and transmit the received electrical power to the electrical power load, responsive to the received indication. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the output to couple to an electrical power load comprises the output to couple to an electrical power termination point which in turn is coupled to the electrical power load, wherein the electrical power termination point is selected from a group consisting of: an electrical receptacle, a disconnect switch, a circuit breaker, a junction box, and a branch circuit. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the control module comprises an Internet of Things (loT) interface via which to transmit the signal to the electrical power load to 

 determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power, and to receive the indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the AC electrical power source is selected from a group consisting of an electrical power grid AC electrical power source, and an electrical power distribution system AC electrical power source. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the AC electrical power source comprises an AC electrical power output of an electrical power distribution system and the DC electrical power source comprises a DC electrical power output of the electrical power distribution system. 6. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the control module to transmit a signal to the electrical power load to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power comprises the control module to transmit a plurality of positive voltage pulses and a plurality of negative voltage pulses to the electrical load; and wherein the control module to receive an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal comprises the control module to: measure an electrical current flow to the electrical power load each transmission of a positive voltage pulse and a negative voltage pulse; 

 compare the electrical current flows associated with the positive voltage pulses with the electrical current flows associated with the negative voltage pulses; detecting the electrical power load uses AC electrical power when the electrical current flows associated with the positive voltage pulses are essentially symmetrical with the electrical current flows associated with the negative voltage pulses; and detecting the electrical power load uses DC electrical power when the electrical current flows associated with the positive voltage pulses are essentially asymmetrical with the electrical current flows associated with the negative voltage pulses. 7. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the control module to transmit a signal to the electrical power load to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power comprises the control module to transmit a plurality of increasingly larger positive voltage pulses to the electrical load; and wherein the control module to receive an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal comprises the control module to: measure an electrical current flow to the electrical power load each transmission of a positive voltage pulse; detecting the electrical power load uses AC electrical power when the electrical current flows associated with the positive voltage pulses are essentially linear with regard to the increasingly larger positive voltage pulses; and 

 detecting the electrical power load uses DC electrical power when the electrical current flows associated with the positive voltage pulses are essentially nonlinear with regard to the increasingly larger positive voltage pulses. 8. An electrical power distribution system, comprising: a plurality of switches, each having a first input to couple to an AC electrical power source, a second input to couple to a DC electrical power source, and an output to couple to a respective one of a plurality of electrical power loads; a control module to transmit a signal to each of the plurality of electrical power loads to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power; the control module to receive from each of the plurality of electrical power loads an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal; and the control module to cause each of the plurality of switches to receive electrical power from one of the AC electrical power source and the DC electrical power source and transmit the received electrical power to the respective one of the plurality of electrical power loads, responsive to the respective received indication. 9. The electrical power distribution system of claim 8, wherein the control module comprises a controller and a signal transceiver coupled to the controller, and wherein: 

 the controller to cause the signal transceiver to transmit the signal to each of the plurality of electrical power loads to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power; the signal transceiver to receive from each of the plurality of electrical power loads an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal; and the controller to receive the indication from the signal transceiver and cause each of the plurality of switches to receive electrical power from one of the AC electrical power source and the DC electrical power source and transmit the received electrical power to the respective one of the plurality of electrical power loads, responsive to the respective received indication. 10. The electrical power distribution system of claim 9, further comprising a plurality of current sensors each coupled in-line between a respective one of the plurality of switches and the plurality of electrical power loads, and wherein the control module to receive an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal, comprises the current sensor to sense current flow or the absence thereof which indicates the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power and transmit the indication to the control module in response thereto. 11. An apparatus, comprising: a plurality of electrical power termination points, each having a respective switch, wherein the switch has a first input to couple to the AC electrical power source, a 

 second input to couple to the DC electrical power source, and an output to couple to a respective one of a plurality of electrical power loads; an electrical power distribution system comprising: the AC electrical power source; the DC electrical power source; and a control module to transmit a signal to each of the plurality of electrical power loads to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power; the control module to receive from each of the plurality of electrical power loads an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal; and the control module to cause each of the plurality of switches to receive electrical power from one of the AC electrical power source and the DC electrical power source and transmit the received electrical power to the respective one of the plurality of electrical power loads, responsive to the respective received indication. 12. An apparatus, comprising: a plurality of electrical power termination points, each comprising: a respective switch that has a first input to couple to the AC electrical power source, a second input to couple to the DC electrical power source, and an output to couple to a respective one of a plurality of electrical power loads; a control module to transmit a signal to the respective one of the plurality of electrical power loads to determine whether the electrical power load uses AC electrical power or DC electrical power; the control module to receive from the respective one of the plurality of electrical power loads an indication that the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power, in response to the transmitted signal; and the control module to cause the respective switch to receive electrical power from one of the AC electrical power source and the DC electrical power source and transmit the received electrical power to the respective one of the plurality of electrical power loads, responsive to the respective received indication; and an electrical power distribution system comprising: the AC electrical power source; and the DC electrical power source. 13. An apparatus, comprising: switching logic having a first input to couple to an AC electrical power source, a second input to couple to a DC electrical power source, and an output to couple to an electrical power load; 

 means for detecting the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power; and means for controlling the switching logic to receive electrical power from the AC electrical power source or the DC electrical power source responsive to detecting the electrical power load uses one of AC electrical power and DC electrical power.
